methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Kairos Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Late Stage), post-money $1.6B+

data.narrative:
TerraPower is a late-stage nuclear energy company founded by Bill Gates that has raised $1.7B+ against a current post-money valuation of $4.0B+. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of five same-sector peers spanning both public and private stages, including Oklo (public, $10.0B), X-Energy (IPO-stage, $2.0B), Kairos Power (late-stage, $1.6B), Nano Nuclear Energy (public, $1.5B), and Zap Energy (Series D, $1.0B).

Two valuation methods are applicable and triangulated. The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.2x multiplier typical of late-stage advancement to the $4.0B last post-money, yielding $4.8B. The Peer-Median method establishes a median across the five comparable companies of $1.6B, though the peer range spans $1.0B to $10.0B, reflecting significant dispersion in the nuclear energy sector. 

The triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $5.5B (high), with a median of $3.2B. The current $4.0B+ valuation sits within this range and above the peer median, which reflects TerraPower's advanced stage, capital intensity, and founder backing. Key uncertainties include the wide peer valuation dispersion—driven by heterogeneous technology maturity, regulatory pathways, and commercialization timelines—and the appropriateness of the 1.2x stage-progression multiplier in an emerging sector with limited comparable late-stage exits.
